questions.q: What is the primary characteristic of a sole proprietorship?
opts: (A) It is a partnership of two or more individuals
(B) It is a business owned by a single individual
(C) It is a public limited company
(D) It is a private limited company
questions.explanation: Sole proprietorship is a business owned by one individual, hence (B) is the correct answer.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a partnership?
opts: (A) It is owned and controlled by a single individual
(B) It is owned and controlled by two or more individuals
(C) It is a public limited company
(D) It is a private limited company
questions.explanation: Partnership is a business owned and controlled by two or more individuals, hence (B) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the main advantage of a joint stock company?
opts: (A) It is easy to form and register
(B) It has unlimited liability
(C) It can raise capital by issuing shares
(D) It is owned by a single individual
questions.explanation: Joint stock companies can raise capital by issuing shares, hence (C) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the main disadvantage of a sole proprietorship?
opts: (A) It has unlimited liability
(B) It is easy to form and register
(C) It has limited liability
(D) It is difficult to manage
questions.explanation: Sole proprietorship has unlimited liability, hence (A) is the correct answer.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a cooperative society?
opts: (A) It is owned and controlled by the government
(B) It is owned and controlled by its members
(C) It is a public limited company
(D) It is a private limited company
questions.explanation: Cooperative society is owned and controlled by its members, hence (B) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a memorandum of association?
opts: (A) To define the objectives of the company
(B) To define the powers of the directors
(C) To define the rights and duties of the shareholders
(D) To define the responsibilities of the management
questions.explanation: Memorandum of association defines the objectives of the company, hence (A) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the difference between a partnership deed and a partnership agreement?
opts: (A) A partnership deed is a written agreement, while a partnership agreement is an oral agreement
(B) A partnership deed is an oral agreement, while a partnership agreement is a written agreement
(C) A partnership deed is a document that defines the rights and duties of partners
(D) A partnership agreement is a document that defines the rights and duties of partners
questions.explanation: Partnership deed is a document that defines the rights and duties of partners, hence (C) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the main advantage of a private limited company?
opts: (A) It has unlimited liability
(B) It has limited liability
(C) It can raise capital by issuing shares
(D) It is easy to form and register
questions.explanation: Private limited company has limited liability, hence (B) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a articles of association?
opts: (A) To define the objectives of the company
(B) To define the powers of the directors
(C) To define the rights and duties of the shareholders
(D) To define the rules and regulations of the company
questions.explanation: Articles of association define the rules and regulations of the company, hence (D) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the main disadvantage of a joint stock company?
opts: (A) It has limited liability
(B) It has unlimited liability
(C) It is easy to form and register
(D) It has complex management structure
questions.explanation: Joint stock company has unlimited liability, hence (B) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a board of directors?
opts: (A) To manage the day-to-day operations of the company
(B) To make strategic decisions for the company
(C) To ensure compliance with laws and regulations
(D) To represent the company in court
questions.explanation: Board of directors makes strategic decisions for the company, hence (B) is the correct answer.

questions.q: What is the main difference between a partnership and a joint stock company?
opts: (A) Partnership is owned by a single individual, while a joint stock company is owned by two or more individuals
(B) Partnership is owned by two or more individuals, while a joint stock company is owned by a single individual
(C) Partnership has limited liability, while a joint stock company has unlimited liability
(D) Partnership has unlimited liability, while a joint stock company has limited liability
questions.explanation: Partnership is owned by two or more individuals, while a joint stock company is owned by a single individual, hence (B) is the correct answer.

opts: (A) To define the objectives of the company
(B) To define the powers of the directors
(C) To define the rights and duties of the shareholders
(D) To define the rules and regulations of the company
questions.explanation: Memorandum of association defines the objectives of the company, hence (A) is the correct answer.

opts: (A) It has limited liability

(D) It is easy to form and register

questions.q: What is the main difference between a public limited company and a private limited company?
opts: (A) Public limited company is owned by a single individual, while a private limited company is owned by two or more individuals
(B) Public limited company is owned by two or more individuals, while a private limited company is owned by a single individual
(C) Public limited company has unlimited liability, while a private limited company has limited liability
(D) Public limited company has limited liability, while a private limited company has unlimited liability
questions.topic: Public Limited Company vs Private Limited Company
questions.explanation: Public limited company has limited liability, while a private limited company has unlimited liability, hence (D) is the correct answer.

opts: (A) To define the objectives of the company

(C) To define the rights and duties of the shareholders
(D) To define the rules and regulations of the company
